rawText:
Noma became famous by showing that a restaurant could build a global reputation from local ingredients, strict seasonality, and a carefully staged sense of discovery. Its cooks explained dishes in the dining room, published influential books, and helped turn Copenhagen into a symbolic capital of modern fine dining. For ambitious young chefs around the world, even a short stay there looked like a passport into the highest tier of restaurant culture.

Yet the same institution that sold freshness, imagination, and moral seriousness also normalized a much older story about labor behind beauty. Former employees said that fear, public shaming, and physical punishment shaped daily life in the kitchen, making excellence feel tied to silence and pain. Many interns once worked for little or no pay, even though the restaurant's fame depended on their exacting work, invisible discipline, and exhausting hours. Its public language celebrated sustainability, collaboration, and the future of food, which made the private accounts of abuse feel especially jarring.

Because Noma influenced so many restaurants, its internal culture never stayed internal for long, and its methods traveled with its prestige. Other kitchens copied its visual style, its devotion to foraging and fermentation, and sometimes its belief that brutal pressure was simply the cost of greatness. Diners were invited to admire the labor behind each plate, but they were rarely asked who paid for that labor with fear, shame, or fatigue. That is what makes the scandal larger than one chef's misconduct: a place with enough authority to reform the trade instead helped preserve some of its ugliest habits.

After allegations resurfaced this year, sponsors retreated from Noma's Los Angeles residency, protesters gathered outside, and René Redzepi announced that he would step down. Yet a resignation by itself does not settle the deeper question of accountability, because a new title cannot undo years of learned behavior or erase a culture people were trained to accept. If Noma once taught the world that a plate could carry a philosophy, it now faces a harder test of whether that philosophy can protect the people who make the meal.

sentences.original: Noma became famous by showing that a restaurant could build a global reputation from local ingredients, strict seasonality, and a carefully staged sense of discovery.
sentences.translation: 노마는 지역 식재료, 엄격한 계절성, 그리고 정교하게 연출된 발견의 감각만으로도 한 레스토랑이 세계적 명성을 쌓을 수 있음을 보여 주며 유명해졌다.

grammarElements.text: that a restaurant could build a global reputation
grammarElements.label: showing의 목적절

vocabulary.example: The menu changes with seasonality rather than fixed supply chains.

expressions.usage: 오랜 시간에 걸쳐 국제적 평판을 만들 때 쓴다

patterns.explanation: 어떤 결과에 이르게 된 방식이나 수단을 by ~ing로 덧붙이는 패턴
examples: The brand became trusted by keeping its promises.
The city became wealthy by attracting trade.

drillQuestions.question: Noma became famous by ______ that a restaurant could build a global reputation.

sentences.notes: 첫 문장에서 by ~ing를 써서 노마의 명성이 어떤 방식으로 형성되었는지 압축적으로 제시한다.
pronunciationNotes: seasonality: /ˌsiːzənˈæləti/ - 강세가 -nal-이 아니라 -al- 앞쪽에 온다는 점을 의식하면 좋다.

sentences.original: Its cooks explained dishes in the dining room, published influential books, and helped turn Copenhagen into a symbolic capital of modern fine dining.
sentences.translation: 그곳의 요리사들은 다이닝룸에서 요리를 설명했고, 영향력 있는 책을 출간했으며, 코펜하겐을 현대 파인다이닝의 상징적 수도로 바꾸는 데 힘을 보탰다.

vocabulary.example: The city became a symbolic center of the movement.

expressions.usage: 어떤 장소나 상태가 다른 성격을 갖게 되었음을 말할 때 유용하다

patterns.explanation: 주어의 여러 행동을 병렬로 나열하며 영향력을 압축하는 패턴
examples: She designed the system, trained the team, and improved the workflow.
They raised funds, hired engineers, and launched the product.

drillQuestions.question: Its cooks explained dishes, published influential books, and helped ______ Copenhagen into a symbolic capital.

sentences.notes: 세 개의 동사를 병렬로 배치해 노마의 문화적 영향력이 한 방향이 아니라 여러 채널로 퍼졌음을 보여 준다.

sentences.original: For ambitious young chefs around the world, even a short stay there looked like a passport into the highest tier of restaurant culture.
sentences.translation: 전 세계의 야심 찬 젊은 셰프들에게 그곳에서의 짧은 경험조차 레스토랑 문화의 최상위권으로 들어가는 여권처럼 보였다.

grammarElements.text: For ambitious young chefs around the world
grammarElements.label: 대상 전치사구

grammarElements.text: a passport into the highest tier of restaurant culture
grammarElements.label: 비유적 보어

vocabulary.example: Only a few schools enter the top tier of the rankings.

expressions.usage: 어떤 경험이 상위 세계로 진입하는 통로처럼 느껴질 때 쓴다

patterns.explanation: 특정 집단이 어떤 경험을 어떻게 인식했는지 비유로 설명하는 패턴
examples: For interns, the project looked like a doorway to a career.
For voters, the speech looked like a promise of change.

drillQuestions.question: Even a short stay there looked like a passport into the highest ______ of restaurant culture.

sentences.notes: passport라는 은유를 통해 노마의 경력이 경력 사다리에서 얼마나 상징적이었는지 드러낸다.
pronunciationNotes: ambitious: /æmˈbɪʃəs/ - 두 번째 음절에 강세가 온다.

sentences.original: Yet the same institution that sold freshness, imagination, and moral seriousness also normalized a much older story about labor behind beauty.
sentences.translation: 하지만 신선함, 상상력, 도덕적 진지함을 내세운 바로 그 기관은 아름다움 뒤의 노동에 관한 훨씬 오래된 이야기 또한 정상화했다.

grammarElements.text: that sold freshness, imagination, and moral seriousness
grammarElements.label: 관계절

grammarElements.text: a much older story about labor behind beauty
grammarElements.label: 목적어

vocabulary.example: Bad habits can become normalized in competitive workplaces.

expressions.usage: 겉으로 보이는 결과를 떠받치는 보이지 않는 수고를 말할 때 쓴다

patterns.pattern: the same [noun] that [positive image] also [negative reality]
patterns.explanation: 겉모습과 실상을 대비시키는 구조로 자주 쓰인다
examples: The same system that promised fairness also rewarded secrecy.
The same app that felt simple also collected too much data.

drillQuestions.question: The same institution ... also ______ a much older story about labor behind beauty.

sentences.notes: same institution과 also를 함께 써서 기대와 현실 사이의 강한 대비를 만든다.

sentences.original: Former employees said that fear, public shaming, and physical punishment shaped daily life in the kitchen, making excellence feel tied to silence and pain.
sentences.translation: 전직 직원들은 두려움, 공개적 망신 주기, 신체적 처벌이 주방의 일상을 규정했고, 그 결과 탁월함이 침묵과 고통에 묶인 것처럼 느껴졌다고 말했다.

grammarElements.text: that fear, public shaming, and physical punishment shaped daily life in the kitchen
grammarElements.label: that절

grammarElements.text: making excellence feel tied to silence and pain
grammarElements.label: 결과를 나타내는 분사구

vocabulary.example: Public shaming can silence people faster than direct orders.

expressions.usage: 어떤 가치나 결과가 불편한 요소와 연결된 느낌을 말할 때 쓴다

patterns.explanation: 증언이나 보도를 간결하게 전달하는 저널리즘 패턴
examples: Workers said that long delays shaped the whole project.
Residents said that rising rents shaped daily choices.

drillQuestions.question: Former employees said that fear, public shaming, and physical punishment ______ daily life in the kitchen.

sentences.notes: 분사구문을 이용해 단순한 사실 보고를 넘어 정서적 결과까지 한 문장 안에 묶는다.

sentences.original: Many interns once worked for little or no pay, even though the restaurant's fame depended on their exacting work, invisible discipline, and exhausting hours.
sentences.translation: 많은 인턴들은 한때 거의 무급이나 다름없이 일했는데, 그 레스토랑의 명성은 바로 그들의 까다로운 노동, 보이지 않는 규율, 그리고 소모적인 장시간 근무에 기대고 있었다.

grammarElements.text: even though the restaurant's fame depended on their exacting work
grammarElements.label: 양보절

vocabulary.example: The role required exacting attention to detail.

expressions.usage: 성과나 상태가 어떤 기반에 기대고 있음을 설명할 때 기본적으로 쓰는 표현

patterns.explanation: 겉으로 드러난 사실과 그 뒤의 역설을 together 보여 주는 양보 구조
examples: The company expanded quickly, even though profits stayed weak.
She kept smiling, even though the pressure was intense.

drillQuestions.question: Many interns once worked for little or no pay, even though the restaurant's fame ______ on their work.

sentences.notes: even though로 겉의 명성과 뒤의 착취 사이의 역설을 뚜렷하게 강조한다.

sentences.original: Its public language celebrated sustainability, collaboration, and the future of food, which made the private accounts of abuse feel especially jarring.
sentences.translation: 그곳의 대외적 언어는 지속가능성, 협업, 그리고 음식의 미래를 찬양했기에, 사적인 학대 증언은 더욱 충격적으로 들렸다.

grammarElements.text: sustainability, collaboration, and the future of food
grammarElements.label: 병렬 목적어

grammarElements.text: which made the private accounts of abuse feel especially jarring
grammarElements.label: 결과를 나타내는 관계절

vocabulary.example: The contrast between the speech and the facts was jarring.

expressions.usage: 식문화의 방향성과 혁신을 말할 때 자주 쓰는 표현

patterns.explanation: 겉으로 보이는 메시지가 다른 사실을 더 충격적으로 만드는 구조
examples: The campaign promised unity, which made the later split feel sudden.
The calm introduction made the final warning feel harsher.

drillQuestions.question: Its public language celebrated sustainability ... which made the private accounts of abuse feel especially ______.

sentences.notes: public/private의 대비를 통해 브랜드 이미지와 내부 현실의 간극을 드러낸다.

sentences.original: Because Noma influenced so many restaurants, its internal culture never stayed internal for long, and its methods traveled with its prestige.
sentences.translation: 노마가 너무 많은 레스토랑에 영향을 미쳤기 때문에, 그 내부 문화는 오래 내부에만 머물지 않았고 그 방식들은 그 명성과 함께 퍼져 나갔다.

grammarElements.text: Because Noma influenced so many restaurants
grammarElements.label: 이유절

grammarElements.text: its methods traveled with its prestige
grammarElements.label: 병렬 절

vocabulary.example: Prestige can hide serious problems if people admire the brand too much.

expressions.usage: 아이디어나 습관이 영향력과 함께 이동할 때 비유적으로 쓴다

patterns.explanation: 이유 하나가 두 가지 결과를 낳는 구조를 보여 준다
examples: Because demand rose quickly, prices climbed and supply tightened.
Because trust fell, customers left and investors worried.

drillQuestions.question: Because Noma influenced so many restaurants, its methods traveled with its ______.

sentences.notes: 영향력 있는 조직의 내부 관행이 외부 산업으로 번질 수 있다는 인과관계를 선명하게 보여 준다.

sentences.original: Other kitchens copied its visual style, its devotion to foraging and fermentation, and sometimes its belief that brutal pressure was simply the cost of greatness.
sentences.translation: 다른 주방들은 그 시각적 스타일과 채집 및 발효에 대한 헌신뿐 아니라, 때로는 잔혹한 압박이 위대함의 대가일 뿐이라는 믿음까지 모방했다.

grammarElements.text: its visual style, its devotion to foraging and fermentation
grammarElements.label: 병렬 목적어

grammarElements.text: that brutal pressure was simply the cost of greatness
grammarElements.label: belief의 내용절

vocabulary.example: Foraging became a symbol of authenticity in many trendy kitchens.

expressions.usage: 성공을 위해 감수해야 한다고 여겨지는 희생을 비판적으로 말할 때 자주 쓴다

patterns.pattern: [Others] copied not only [visible traits] but also [harmful belief]
patterns.explanation: 겉으로 드러난 요소와 보이지 않는 태도를 함께 전염되는 것으로 설명할 때 쓰는 패턴
examples: Competitors copied the design and the culture of overwork.
Students copied the format and the fear of making mistakes.

drillQuestions.question: Other kitchens copied its devotion to foraging and ______.

sentences.notes: 겉으로 멋있어 보이는 기법과 해로운 사고방식이 함께 복제되었다는 점을 한 문장에 묶는다.

sentences.original: Diners were invited to admire the labor behind each plate, but they were rarely asked who paid for that labor with fear, shame, or fatigue.
sentences.translation: 손님들은 각 접시 뒤의 노동을 감탄하도록 초대받았지만, 그 노동의 대가를 누가 두려움과 수치심, 피로로 치렀는지는 거의 묻지 않았다.

grammarElements.text: to admire the labor behind each plate
grammarElements.label: to부정사 보충어

grammarElements.text: but they were rarely asked who paid for that labor
grammarElements.label: 대조절

vocabulary.example: Fatigue often accumulates long before workers admit it.

expressions.usage: 공개적으로 어떤 시선을 기대하거나 장려할 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.pattern: [People] were invited to [positive response], but [hidden question]
patterns.explanation: 겉으로 허용된 반응과 배제된 질문을 대조할 때 쓰는 패턴
examples: Viewers were invited to celebrate the launch, but few asked about the cost.
Guests were invited to relax, but staff were never asked how tired they were.

drillQuestions.question: Diners were invited to admire the labor behind each plate, but they were rarely asked who ______ for that labor.

sentences.notes: 수동태를 사용해 손님에게 허용된 시선과 금지된 질문을 자연스럽게 대비시킨다.

sentences.original: That is what makes the scandal larger than one chef's misconduct: a place with enough authority to reform the trade instead helped preserve some of its ugliest habits.
sentences.translation: 바로 그 점이 이 스캔들을 한 셰프의 비행보다 더 큰 문제로 만든다. 업계를 개혁할 만큼 충분한 권위를 지닌 곳이 오히려 가장 추한 관행 일부를 보존하는 데 힘을 보탰기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: the scandal larger than one chef's misconduct
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + 비교 구조

grammarElements.text: a place with enough authority to reform the trade instead helped preserve some of its ugliest habits
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 설명절

vocabulary.example: The report argued that the problem was systemic, not just personal misconduct.

expressions.usage: 제도나 조직의 영향력을 설명할 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.pattern: That is what makes [issue] larger than [narrow explanation]
patterns.explanation: 문제의 범위를 개인 수준에서 구조 수준으로 확장할 때 쓰는 패턴
examples: That is what makes the crisis larger than one policy mistake.
That is what makes the debate larger than one bad headline.

drillQuestions.question: That is what makes the scandal larger than one chef's ______.

sentences.notes: 콜론을 사용해 핵심 주장 뒤에 바로 구조적 설명을 붙이며 논지를 강화한다.

sentences.original: After allegations resurfaced this year, sponsors retreated from Noma's Los Angeles residency, protesters gathered outside, and René Redzepi announced that he would step down.
sentences.translation: 올해 이 의혹들이 다시 떠오른 뒤, 후원사들은 노마의 로스앤젤레스 레지던시에서 물러났고 시위대는 바깥에 모였으며 르네 레드제피는 자신이 물러나겠다고 발표했다.

grammarElements.text: sponsors retreated, protesters gathered, and René Redzepi announced
grammarElements.label: 병렬 서술

vocabulary.example: The company planned a three-month residency in another city.

expressions.usage: 리더가 자리에서 사임하거나 직책을 내려놓을 때 자주 쓰는 표현

patterns.explanation: 하나의 사건 이후에 연쇄 반응이 일어났음을 빠르게 요약하는 뉴스형 패턴
examples: After the report broke, shares fell, executives met, and regulators responded.
After the storm hit, flights stopped, roads closed, and schools shut.

drillQuestions.question: René Redzepi announced that he would ______ down.

sentences.notes: 병렬 구조가 사건 이후의 압박을 빠르게 압축하며 뉴스 기사 같은 리듬을 만든다.

sentences.original: Yet a resignation by itself does not settle the deeper question of accountability, because a new title cannot undo years of learned behavior or erase a culture people were trained to accept.
sentences.translation: 하지만 사임만으로는 더 깊은 책임의 문제를 해결할 수 없다. 새로운 직함이 수년간 학습된 행동을 되돌리거나 사람들이 받아들이도록 훈련된 문화를 지울 수는 없기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: because a new title cannot undo years of learned behavior or erase a culture people were trained to accept
grammarElements.label: 이유절

vocabulary.example: Public accountability requires more than a symbolic apology.

expressions.usage: 어떤 조치 하나가 충분하지 않음을 말할 때 매우 자주 쓰는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Action] by itself does not [solve issue], because [deeper reason]
patterns.explanation: 표면적 조치와 구조적 한계를 구분하는 데 유용한 논증 패턴
examples: A warning by itself does not change the system, because incentives stay the same.
A policy by itself does not build trust, because people remember past failures.

drillQuestions.question: A resignation by itself does not settle the deeper question of ______.

sentences.notes: by itself와 because를 사용해 상징적 조치와 구조적 변화의 차이를 분명하게 짚는다.

sentences.original: If Noma once taught the world that a plate could carry a philosophy, it now faces a harder test of whether that philosophy can protect the people who make the meal.
sentences.translation: 만약 노마가 한때 한 접시가 하나의 철학을 담을 수 있다고 세상에 가르쳤다면, 이제 그 철학이 식사를 만드는 사람들을 보호할 수 있는지라는 더 어려운 시험과 마주하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: If Noma once taught the world
grammarElements.label: 조건절

grammarElements.text: that a plate could carry a philosophy
grammarElements.label: taught의 내용절

grammarElements.text: a harder test of whether that philosophy can protect the people who make the meal
grammarElements.label: 목적어

vocabulary.example: A company can advertise a philosophy, but workers test whether it is real.

expressions.usage: 이전보다 더 본질적인 검증을 받게 될 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.explanation: 과거의 성취를 인정하면서 현재의 더 큰 과제를 제시하는 패턴
examples: If the firm once led the market, it now faces a harder test of trust.
If the law once promised equality, it now faces a harder test of enforcement.

drillQuestions.question: It now faces a harder test of whether that philosophy can ______ the people who make the meal.

sentences.notes: 조건절을 써서 과거의 영광과 현재의 윤리적 과제를 하나의 결론으로 연결한다.

koreanAlignment.en: the people who make the meal
